task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Vicidial System Settings page, the field or dropdown for 'Non-Latin Characters' (use_non_latin) is set to '1'.
In the Vicidial System Settings page, the field or dropdown for 'Custom Fields' (custom_fields_enabled) is set to '1'.
In the Vicidial System Settings page, the field or dropdown for 'Allow Chats' (allow_chats) is set to '1'.
In the Vicidial System Settings page, the field or input box for 'Callback Limit' is set to '1'.
In the Vicidial System Settings page, the field or dropdown for 'QueueMetrics Logging' (enable_queuemetrics_logging) is set to '1'.
In the Vicidial System Settings page, the field or dropdown for 'Allow Emails' (allow_emails) is set to '1'.

task_completion.description: Save or submit the updated system settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Submit' or 'Save' button on the System Settings page, and/or a confirmation message is visible indicating that the system settings have been successfully updated.

integrity.description: Agent used the Vicidial web interface to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Vicidial web administration GUI in a browser, rather than executing MySQL queries or terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely modified the form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The form fields should be modified through standard browser interaction (clicking dropdowns, typing in text boxes), not by editing the DOM via browser developer tools.

integrity.description: Agent did not use external scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent using curl, python scripts, or other programmatic methods in a terminal to submit the HTTP POST request directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target values for use_non_latin, custom_fields_enabled, allow_chats, callback_limit, enable_queuemetrics_logging, and allow_emails must all be exactly '1'. These fields are initially set to '0' in the database.
